company.name: Sieve
company.notes: AI video/image API. Newer, growing fast.

research.status_summary: Actively operating and hiring — Sieve pivoted in early 2026 from a developer-facing video API platform to a full multimodal data lab supplying petabyte-scale training datasets to frontier AI labs, with 8 open roles on YC as of mid-2026.
research.hiring_policy: Roles listed on YC and Wellfound are in-office San Francisco at $150k–$300k, suggesting no remote policy and likely no EOR-friendliness; no public mention of visa sponsorship found.
recent_news: March 4, 2026: Published 'Reintroducing Sieve' blog post detailing the pivot from video APIs to supplying multimodal data at petabyte-scale to frontier AI labs and Fortune 100 companies.

Sieve now operates on hundreds of petabytes of video (every 100 PB ≈ 75M hours of 1080p content) with fully software-driven collection, indexing, filtering, QA, and annotation systems — not manual service workflows.
The company's current product scope spans media generation/editing, visual understanding, robotics, world models, and computer use datasets, moving well beyond its original video-editing API roots.
research.growth_signal: Headcount sits at ~18–22 employees (per YC and PitchBook), with $12M total raised (per StartupHub, detected May 2026), 8 active YC job listings, and rapidly expanding scope from single-product API to full multimodal data infrastructure lab serving frontier AI customers.
research.fit_for_lior: Strong fit for Lior's Creative AI and Vibe Coder threads — Sieve sits at the infrastructure layer powering generative video/image models, and its developer-facing API roots (dubbing, lipsync, scene detection, auto-crop) offer natural overlap with creative tooling and product experimentation.
